frameworks.name: event schema / situation model
frameworks.one_line: high-level situation model abstracts narrative events using schematic scripts

frameworks.name: Hidden Markov Model
frameworks.one_line: HMM used to identify and align schematic event states in fMRI patterns

frameworks.name: event segmentation theory
frameworks.one_line: narrative comprehension as segmentation into discrete events

core_question: Which brain regions represent abstract event schemas that generalize across stories, modalities, and subjects during narrative perception?
key_assumptions: comprehension depends on prior knowledge of schematic event scripts
schematic representations abstract away from perceptual surface details
shared script structure produces shared neural patterns across stories
manipulations: presentation modality (audiovisual clips vs spoken narration)
temporal order (intact sequence vs scrambled events)
key_findings: posterior medial cortex, mPFC, and superior frontal gyrus show schematic event patterns generalizing across stories, subjects, and modalities
HMM-derived patterns predict the underlying script of unlabeled data with high accuracy
shared schematic patterns enable temporal alignment of multiple stories with a common script
interpretations: these regions encode abstract event schemas independent of surface narrative form
schematic scripts shape neural representations during real-world narrative perception
